architecture_summary: DeiT is a vision transformer architecture identical to ViT-B with no convolutions, trained on Imagenet only with strong data augmentation and a transformer-specific distillation token. The paper also introduces smaller variants DeiT-S and DeiT-Ti, and uses bicubic interpolation for fine-tuning at higher resolution.

training_details.compute_surface: single 8-GPU node in two to three days (53 hours of pre-training, and optionally 20 hours of fine-tuning)

novel_contributions: We introduce a teacher-student strategy specific to transformers based on a distillation token ensuring that the student learns from the teacher through attention.
We show that convolution-free vision transformers can be trained on Imagenet only, on a single computer in less than 3 days, with competitive results and no external data.
We introduce DeiT-S and DeiT-Ti as smaller models with fewer parameters that can be seen as the counterpart of ResNet-50 and ResNet-18.
Our transformer-specific distillation strategy outperforms vanilla distillation by a significant margin.
Our models pre-learned on Imagenet are competitive when transferred to different downstream tasks such as CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, Oxford-102 flowers, Stanford Cars and iNaturalist-18/19.

key_claims.claim: The reference vision transformer achieves 83.1% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet with 86M parameters and no external data.
The paper trains convolution-free transformers on Imagenet only, on a single computer in less than 3 days.
The distillation token lets the student learn from the teacher through attention and improves over vanilla distillation.
The best model on ImageNet reaches up to 85.2% accuracy and is competitive with convnets.
DeiT-B★ †384 / 1000 epochs achieves 85.2 top-1 on ImageNet and 89.3 on ImageNet Real.
key_claims.evidence_section: 5.3 Efficiency vs accuracy: a comparative study with convnets

key_claims.claim: DeiT models transfer well to downstream tasks and are competitive with convnet models on several public benchmarks.
key_claims.evidence_section: 5.4 Transfer learning: Performance on downstream tasks

key_claims.claim: Strong data augmentation, repeated augmentation, stochastic depth, Mixup, Cutmix, and careful optimization are key ingredients for data-efficient training.
